item_text: the diagram models the process of two users exchanging information to determine if they have a match.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-matchdetection-001 title: two users successfully detect a match module: match detection source pages: 7 preconditions: both users have been registered and have swiped each other's profiles test data: - user1: testuser1 - user2: testuser2 test steps: 1. user1 swipes right on user2's profile. 2. application sends a preference tracking request to the server. 3. server saves the preference in the database. 4. server checks for a match and sends a response to user1. 5. user2 swipes right on user1's profile. 6. application sends a preference tracking request to the server. 7. server saves the preference in the database. 8. server checks for a match and sends a response to user1. 9. user1 receives a match notification. expected result: user1 sees a match notification with user2.

item_text: the process involves sending preferences, checking for matches, and confirming the results.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-matchdetection-001 title: two users successfully detect a match module: match detection source pages: 7 preconditions: both users have been registered and have swiped each other's profiles test data: - user1: testuser1 - user2: testuser2 test steps: 1. user1 swipes right on user2's profile. 2. application sends a preference tracking request to the server. 3. server saves the preference in the database. 4. server checks for a match and sends a response to user1. 5. user2 swipes right on user1's profile. 6. application sends a preference tracking request to the server. 7. server saves the preference in the database. 8. server checks for a match and sends a response to user1. 9. user1 receives a match notification. expected result: user1 sees a match notification with user2.
